x-enumeration-literals.name: Payer
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payer (Sender) The party initiating the cash transfer to fulfill a financial obligation or make a payment.

Comment:
Example:
A customer sending €500 to pay a supplier.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Payee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payee (Receiver / Beneficiary) The party receiving the funds from the transfer.

Comment:
Example:
A supplier receiving €500 for goods delivered.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Bank
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Bank or Financial Institution (Sending Bank) The bank that executes the transfer on behalf of the payer.

Comment:
Example:
Bank A processes a domestic wire transfer.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Intermediary Bank
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Receiving Bank or Intermediary Bank: The bank that receives the transferred funds and credits the payee, especially in external or international transfers.

Comment:
Example:
Bank B in another country receiving international remittance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Payment Processor
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An intermediary or network that facilitates the transfer, clearing, and settlement of funds.

Comment:
Example:
SWIFT network for international wire transfers
SEPA for European euro transfers
Automated Clearing House (ACH) for batch transfers
